text: New paper out: sunflower sea stars are recolonizing parts of the WA coast faster than we modeled. Cautious optimism, with caveats in the thread. 🧵
4:50am, minus 3.1ft tide at Salt Creek. Worth every bit of lost sleep. Gumboot chiton the size of my hand. 📷
Big minus tides this week across south Puget Sound. Thu 10/8 hits -2.7ft around 11:40am near Tacoma. Great window if you can get out.
Summer heat dome left visible scars in the upper intertidal again this year. Bleached barnacle bands, mussel die-off above the +2ft line. We're writing it up.
Today's slug: the opalescent nudibranch (Hermissenda crassicornis). Those orange tips are not for subtlety.
The herring are spawning and the whole nearshore knows it. Gulls, sea lions, and one very smug heron. Spring in the Salish Sea is loud.
field day at site 7 done, waders only partially flooded, found three species i'd never logged here before. good morning honestly 🌊
Reminder: if you see a sea star with lesions or losing arms, log it on our portal. Recovery data only works if we all report. 🌟
me, explaining my thesis to a relative at thanksgiving: so the little ocean bugs are. moving. north. and that's bad. *they nod politely*
your advisor: 'just a quick edit' the document:
draft chapter 2 = officially a real document with real words in it. it is bad. but it EXISTS. progress 🎉
Atmospheric river lining up for the weekend. South Sound could see 2-3in of rain Sat into Sun. Wind picks up Sunday afternoon. Tie down the tarps.
Record green crab catch in a single Willapa Bay trap line this month. This is not the milestone we wanted. Report your sightings, please.
Thread: a year of tide pools at one beach, month by month. Same square meter, totally different worlds. 1/12 - January, everything tucked in tight against the cold. 📷
Our 2026 nearshore monitoring report is live. Eelgrass holding steady in most sites, declines at two south Sound stations. Full PDF linked.
There is a specific gray the sky turns in October here, like the inside of an oyster shell. I have been trying to write one good sentence about it for an hour.
Spent the afternoon in the collections drawer reidentifying chitons from a 1978 survey. Two were mislabeled. History is a living thing, even in jars.
@drlenacarvalho congrats on the sea star paper! we're seeing early juvenile recruitment at our south Sound sites too. cautiously thrilled.
Leading a free tide pool walk at Titlow Beach this Saturday, low tide ~10am. Bring layers and curiosity. We never run out of things to flip over (gently).
Paddled out of Gig Harbor at dusk and the whole wake lit up neon blue. Bioluminescence season is the best kept secret in the south Sound.
New taco truck parked off 6th Ave and the al pastor is genuinely dangerous. Cash only, open til 9. You're welcome.
PSA before you head to the wild coast this weekend: check the tide tables TWICE. People get cliffed out at Shi Shi every single year. The ocean does not negotiate.
tacoma gave us one (1) clear evening and the whole sky over commencement bay went pink. ran outside in socks. no regrets
day 3 of fieldwork, the waders have officially betrayed me, found a sea slug that looks like a tiny dragon. net positive.
Saturday salmon habitat planting day on the Puyallup was a soggy success. 400 native plants in the ground, 30 volunteers, infinite mud. Thank you all. 🌧️
